evals.prompt: hey so i just inherited this java spring boot microservice (in ./mock-repos/payment-service) from a dev who left and my manager wants me to figure out if we're using schema registry properly. can you scan it and tell me what's going on? i think they're producing to some payment topics but i'm not sure if we have schemas registered anywhere. also if there's any pii in the data that would be good to know since we have a compliance audit coming up
evals.expected_output: Full schema-report.md identifying producers, extracting schemas from Java data models, tagging PII fields (credit card, email, etc.), generating Terraform configs, and providing upgrade recommendations

assertions.name: schema_report_exists
assertions.description: schema-report.md file was created

assertions.name: schemas_directory_exists
assertions.description: schemas/ directory was created

assertions.name: correct_number_of_schemas
assertions.description: 2 schema files generated (payment-events-value, payment-confirmations-value)

assertions.name: terraform_directory_exists
assertions.description: terraform/ directory was created

assertions.name: terraform_files_present
assertions.description: Core Terraform files present (providers.tf, variables.tf, schemas.tf, tags.tf, outputs.tf)

assertions.name: pii_fields_tagged_email
assertions.description: customer_email field tagged with PII in schema

assertions.name: pii_fields_tagged_credit_card
assertions.description: credit_card_number field tagged with PII and PRIVATE

assertions.name: report_identifies_category_b
assertions.description: Report correctly identifies Category B (schema in code, no SR)

assertions.name: report_contains_pii_summary
assertions.description: Report contains PII fields section with at least 3 PII fields

assertions.name: upgrade_recommendations_present
assertions.description: Report contains upgrade recommendations for Spring JsonSerializer

evals.prompt: I need to analyze our order processing repo for Kafka usage. The path is ./mock-repos/order-processor and it's a Python app using confluent-kafka. We've been having issues where schema changes break consumers and I suspect someone enabled auto-registration. Can you scan the whole thing, extract any schemas you find, and generate terraform so we can manage schemas properly? We're using Confluent Cloud btw
evals.expected_output: Report identifying auto.register.schemas=true risks, Python data models (Pydantic/dataclasses), extracted schemas in schemas/json/, Terraform with flagged-auto-register.tf commented out, rollout ordering guidance

assertions.name: auto_register_risk_detected
assertions.description: Report identifies auto.register.schemas=true as a risk

assertions.name: auto_register_locations_found
assertions.description: Report identifies both locations (config.yaml and producer.py)

assertions.name: schemas_extracted_from_pydantic
assertions.description: 2 schemas extracted from Pydantic models (OrderCreated, OrderUpdated)

assertions.name: flagged_auto_register_tf_exists
assertions.description: flagged-auto-register.tf file created

assertions.name: flagged_schemas_commented_out
assertions.description: Schema resources in flagged-auto-register.tf are commented out

assertions.name: risk_section_in_report
assertions.description: Report has RISKS section highlighting auto-registration

assertions.name: category_c_identified
assertions.description: Application categorized as Category C (auto-register)

assertions.name: migration_steps_provided
assertions.description: Report provides migration steps to disable auto-registration

evals.prompt: our main backend is at ./mock-repos/ecommerce-platform and it has kafka stuff in multiple languages - java for the order service, python for analytics, and some node.js microservices. I want to understand what topics we're producing to, what serializers we're using, and get everything into schema registry with terraform. also tag any customer data that might be PII since we need to be gdpr compliant
evals.expected_output: Multi-language analysis covering Java/Python/Node producers, categorizing each by serializer type, extracting schemas from different data models across languages, consolidated Terraform configs, PII tagging summary table

assertions.name: three_services_identified
assertions.description: Report identifies 3 Kafka services (order, analytics, notification)

assertions.name: three_languages_detected
assertions.description: Report identifies Java, Python, and Node.js/TypeScript

assertions.name: three_schemas_extracted
assertions.description: 3 schemas extracted (orders, analytics-events, notifications)

assertions.name: pii_fields_tagged
assertions.description: At least 5 PII fields tagged across schemas (email, phone, address, ip)

assertions.name: gdpr_compliance_mentioned
assertions.description: Report mentions GDPR compliance

assertions.name: consolidated_terraform
assertions.description: Single Terraform config with all 3 schemas

assertions.name: per_service_categorization
assertions.description: Report categorizes each service separately

assertions.name: pii_summary_table
assertions.description: Report contains PII summary table or section

evals.prompt: ok so this is embarrassing but we have a legacy service (./mock-repos/legacy-event-publisher) that's using a custom json serializer - basically just calling json.dumps in python and sending raw bytes. no schema registry at all. we want to migrate to using proper confluent serializers but i need to know what schemas to extract first and how to do the migration without breaking our 5 consumer apps. can you analyze it?
evals.expected_output: Category E identification (custom serializer), schema extraction from inline dict structures, Terraform generation, detailed rollout ordering (consumers first with composite deserializer pattern), migration steps

assertions.name: category_e_identified
assertions.description: Application categorized as Category E (custom serializer)

assertions.name: custom_serializer_detected
assertions.description: Report identifies json.dumps as custom serializer

assertions.name: schemas_extracted_from_dicts
assertions.description: 2 schemas extracted from dict structures (user-events, product-events)

assertions.name: consumers_first_rollout
assertions.description: Report specifies consumers-first rollout ordering

assertions.name: composite_deserializer_mentioned
assertions.description: Report mentions composite/dual-read deserializer pattern for migration

assertions.name: breaking_change_warning
assertions.description: Report warns about breaking changes to 5 consumer apps

assertions.name: migration_steps_detailed
assertions.description: Report provides detailed migration steps

assertions.name: terraform_generated
assertions.description: Terraform schemas.tf generated for both topics

evals.prompt: We're already using schema registry for our kafka streams app in ./mock-repos/streams-processor but we want to move the schema IDs from the message payload to kafka headers to save bandwidth. Can you scan the repo and figure out what we need to change? I think we're on a recent version of confluent platform
evals.expected_output: Category A→Header scenario, report showing current SR integration, recommendation to add HeaderSchemaIdSerializer to producers, verification that consumers are on supported versions (automatic dual-read), no schema extraction needed

assertions.name: current_sr_integration_detected
assertions.description: Report confirms Schema Registry is already configured

assertions.name: header_serializer_recommended
assertions.description: Report recommends HeaderSchemaIdSerializer

assertions.name: version_check_mentioned
assertions.description: Report mentions version requirements (CP 8.1.1+ or similar)

assertions.name: automatic_dual_read_explained
assertions.description: Report explains automatic dual-read behavior of deserializers

assertions.name: no_consumer_changes_needed
assertions.description: Report states consumers don't need changes

assertions.name: bandwidth_savings_mentioned
assertions.description: Report mentions bandwidth savings (5 bytes per message)

assertions.name: no_schemas_directory
assertions.description: No schemas/ directory created (not needed for this scenario)

evals.prompt: analyze the repo at ./mock-repos/notification-service - it's a .NET app using Confluent.Kafka and i know for sure it's sending user emails, phone numbers, and maybe ssns to kafka topics. i need to extract all the schemas, tag the pii fields properly for our data governance team, and get terraform set up. also check if we're using schema registry correctly
evals.expected_output: C# data models analyzed, extensive PII tagging (email, phone, ssn with PRIVATE tag), JSON schemas generated with confluent:tags, terraform/tags.tf with confluent_tag resources, depends_on relationships in schemas.tf

assertions.name: email_pii_tagged
assertions.description: Email field tagged with PII

assertions.name: phone_pii_tagged
assertions.description: Phone number field tagged with PII

assertions.name: ssn_private_tagged
assertions.description: SSN field tagged with both PII and PRIVATE

assertions.name: at_least_8_pii_fields
assertions.description: At least 8 PII fields tagged across schemas

assertions.name: terraform_tags_tf_exists
assertions.description: terraform/tags.tf file created with tag resources

assertions.name: pii_tag_resource_defined
assertions.description: confluent_tag resource for PII defined

assertions.name: private_tag_resource_defined
assertions.description: confluent_tag resource for PRIVATE defined

assertions.name: depends_on_tags
assertions.description: Schema resources have depends_on for tags

assertions.name: not_using_sr_correctly
assertions.description: Report identifies that SR is NOT being used (JsonConvert)

evals.prompt: i have this monorepo at ./mock-repos/acme-services with like 12 different microservices and i think 4 or 5 of them use kafka. can you scan the whole thing, identify which services are producing/consuming, what topics they use, extract schemas for each one, and organize everything so i can see which services need to be upgraded to use schema registry? we're trying to get governance in place before our series B
evals.expected_output: Monorepo analysis with app catalog showing all Kafka services, per-service categorization, deduplicated schemas where same model is used across services, organized Terraform with comments showing which service each schema comes from, prioritized upgrade plan

assertions.name: kafka_services_identified
assertions.description: Report identifies 3-4 Kafka services (user, billing, inventory, reporting)

assertions.name: app_catalog_table
assertions.description: Report contains application catalog table

assertions.name: per_service_categories
assertions.description: Each service has a category assigned

assertions.name: schemas_extracted
assertions.description: At least 3 schemas extracted

assertions.name: terraform_with_service_comments
assertions.description: Terraform schemas.tf has comments identifying which service each schema comes from

assertions.name: upgrade_priority_plan
assertions.description: Report provides prioritized upgrade plan or phased rollout

assertions.name: series_b_readiness
assertions.description: Report mentions Series B or governance readiness

evals.prompt: check out ./mock-repos/go-kafka-producer - it's a golang service that's doing json.Marshal right before producing to kafka. no schema registry. i want to know what the schema looks like based on the structs being serialized, get terraform to register it, and understand what i need to change in the code to use confluent serializers. also we have some sensitive customer data in there
evals.expected_output: Go struct analysis with json:"field" tags, schema inference from struct types, Category B/E classification, Terraform with Go-specific upgrade instructions, PII tagging based on field names, dependency addition guidance (confluent-kafka-go)

assertions.name: go_structs_analyzed
assertions.description: Report identifies CustomerEvent and OrderEvent structs

assertions.name: schemas_inferred_from_structs
assertions.description: 2 schemas generated (customer-events, order-events)

assertions.name: json_tags_preserved
assertions.description: Schema field names match json struct tags (snake_case)

assertions.name: pii_fields_tagged_go
assertions.description: At least 4 PII fields tagged (email, phone_number, first_name, last_name, account_number)

assertions.name: json_marshal_detected
assertions.description: Report identifies json.Marshal as custom serialization

assertions.name: go_upgrade_instructions
assertions.description: Report provides Go-specific upgrade instructions

assertions.name: dependency_guidance
assertions.description: Report mentions adding confluent-kafka-go dependency

assertions.name: sensitive_data_identified
assertions.description: Report acknowledges sensitive customer data

evals.prompt: so we have this weird situation where the same kafka topic (transaction-events) is getting two completely different event types from different services - OrderCreatedEvent from the order service and PaymentProcessedEvent from the payment service. can you scan ./mock-repos/transaction-system and figure out how to handle this with schema registry? i heard something about wrapper schemas
evals.expected_output: Multi-schema topic detection, individual event schema extraction, wrapper schema generation using oneOf (JSON Schema) or union (Avro), Terraform with schema_reference blocks, cross-reference table in report showing which events go to which topics

assertions.name: multi_schema_topic_detected
assertions.description: Report identifies transaction-events as multi-schema topic

assertions.name: two_event_types_identified
assertions.description: Report identifies OrderCreatedEvent and PaymentProcessedEvent

assertions.name: individual_schemas_extracted
assertions.description: Individual event schemas created (not just wrapper)

assertions.name: wrapper_schema_generated
assertions.description: Wrapper/envelope schema created

assertions.name: union_or_oneof_used
assertions.description: Wrapper schema uses union (Avro) or oneOf (JSON Schema)

assertions.name: schema_references_in_terraform
assertions.description: Terraform has schema_reference blocks

assertions.name: cross_reference_table
assertions.description: Report contains cross-reference table showing event types and topics

assertions.name: wrapper_pattern_explained
assertions.description: Report explains wrapper schema pattern

evals.prompt: please analyze the repo at ./mock-repos/api-gateway - i don't think it uses kafka at all but my manager asked me to check. just scan it and let me know if there are any kafka producers or consumers and if we need to do anything with schema registry
